description_long: This object features a distinctive structure with two vertical cylindrical red columns, each anchored by a gray hemispherical base, supporting a wide, flat, black rectangular top. The top surface is slightly curved upward at the ends, reminiscent of traditional Japanese torii gates, but with a modern, electronic twist. A horizontal red beam connects the two columns, providing additional stability and a visual break in the design. The materials appear to be a combination of painted metal or composite for the red and black elements, and concrete or stone for the bases. The object is likely an urban electronic device, possibly a sensor or transmitter, designed to be both functional and visually striking in public spaces. There are no visible screens, buttons, or obvious user interfaces, suggesting it operates autonomously or remotely.
description: A modern, red and black urban electronic structure with two cylindrical columns and a wide, flat top, anchored by gray bases.
description_view_0: A narrow end-on view showing the slim profile of the black top and one cylindrical red column with a gray base.
description_view_1: A full frontal view displaying both red columns, the connecting horizontal beam, and the wide, slightly curved black top.
description_view_2: Another narrow end-on view, mirroring view 0, with the same slim profile and single column visible.
description_view_3: A rear view similar to the front, showing both columns, the horizontal beam, and the black top, but with less detail on the front-facing features.
